behaviors.description: Renamed files are reported only in the renamed category, not double-counted as added+deleted.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: real git rename detection and CLI/subprocess/file-state interaction. A pure parser/unit test can miss failures where the tool invokes git without rename detection or with wrong flags, causing rename pairs to surface as delete+add. Pass 2 mapping: requires real subprocess + real repo state => System. Integration-brake check: needs real subprocess behavior (yes), no AI/ML dependency (no), no multi-agent chain (no). Rejected higher levels: behavior is deterministic and contains no model-quality or multi-agent requirement, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by the behavior text itself.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it with high similarity content, run the CLI against recent changes, and assert the report has the file only under renamed with added/deleted unchanged by that rename.

behaviors.description: Binary-file diffs do not crash the tool and binary files are counted.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: real binary diff output and process/encoding behavior from git. Mocked text fixtures often hide decode/stream/exit-code failures that only appear with actual binary blobs and git output format. Pass 2 mapping: real subprocess + filesystem content characteristics => System. Integration-brake check: real OS/subprocess needed (yes), no AI/ML dependency (no), no multi-step agent pipeline (no). Rejected higher levels: no LLM or workflow semantics in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: In a temporary repo, commit and then change/add a binary file (e.g., image/random bytes), run the CLI, assert zero crash, successful exit, and binary file inclusion in the correct count/category.

behaviors.description: A repository with no commits yields an empty report, not an error.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: real git behavior on unborn HEAD/no-commit repositories, including exit codes/messages. Fakes can miss the exact failure boundary (command failure handling). Pass 2 mapping: real subprocess semantics required => System. Integration-brake check: needs real subprocess/OS interaction (yes), no AI/ML (no), no agentic chain (no). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is deterministic CLI error-handling, not model-dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert successful handling with an empty structured report and no uncaught error.

behaviors.description: Reported counts match the repository’s actual change history.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: end-to-end correctness against real commit history and diff semantics across multiple change types. Mock histories can mask mismatches caused by git command selection/range handling. Pass 2 mapping: requires real repository + git subprocess outputs => System. Integration-brake check: real OS/subprocess needed (yes), no AI/ML dependency (no), no multi-agent chain (no). Rejected higher levels: no nondeterministic model judgment involved.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history with known numbers of added, deleted, renamed, and modified files across commits; run CLI for the target range; assert each reported count equals the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: Output is identical whether analyzed changes are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: real git index vs working-tree semantics and CLI command behavior over those states. In-process mocks often normalize these states and hide real discrepancies. Pass 2 mapping: requires real repo state transitions and subprocess calls => System. Integration-brake check: real OS/subprocess needed (yes), no AI/ML (no), no multi-agent chain (no). Rejected higher levels: deterministic comparison of two git states, not model-based.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create identical logical changes in two scenarios (staged vs unstaged), run CLI in each scenario, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout.
